name: Beam Deflection Calculator
description: Industrial-grade beam deflection analysis tool based on Euler-Bernoulli beam theory, compliant with ISO 9001 and Six Sigma standards. Calculates maximum deflection, bending stress, and safety factors for simply supported beams under various loading conditions.

inputs.label: Beam Length (L)
inputs.businessContext: Span length between supports, typical for structural steel beams in industrial racks (WERC standard).

businessContext_i18n.en: Span length between supports, typical for structural steel beams in industrial racks (WERC standard).

inputs.label: Load Type
inputs.businessContext: Common load configurations in material handling and structural analysis per ISO 9001 design control.

businessContext_i18n.en: Common load configurations in material handling and structural analysis per ISO 9001 design control.

inputs.label: Point Load (P)
inputs.businessContext: Concentrated force applied at a specific point, typical for forklift impact or equipment loads.

businessContext_i18n.en: Concentrated force applied at a specific point, typical for forklift impact or equipment loads.

inputs.label: Uniformly Distributed Load (w)
inputs.businessContext: Load per unit length, e.g., conveyor belt or storage rack loading per linear meter.

businessContext_i18n.en: Load per unit length, e.g., conveyor belt or storage rack loading per linear meter.

inputs.label: Load Position from Left Support (a)
inputs.businessContext: Distance from left support to point load application, used for asymmetric loading analysis.

businessContext_i18n.en: Distance from left support to point load application, used for asymmetric loading analysis.

inputs.label: Young's Modulus (E)
inputs.businessContext: Material stiffness property, typical for steel (200 GPa) or aluminum (70 GPa) per ISO 6892-1.

businessContext_i18n.en: Material stiffness property, typical for steel (200 GPa) or aluminum (70 GPa) per ISO 6892-1.

inputs.label: Moment of Inertia (I)
inputs.businessContext: Cross-sectional property resisting bending, calculated from beam profile (e.g., I-beam, rectangular).

businessContext_i18n.en: Cross-sectional property resisting bending, calculated from beam profile (e.g., I-beam, rectangular).

inputs.label: Yield Strength (σ_y)
inputs.businessContext: Material yield stress, typical for structural steel S235 (235 MPa) or S355 (355 MPa) per EN 10025.

businessContext_i18n.en: Material yield stress, typical for structural steel S235 (235 MPa) or S355 (355 MPa) per EN 10025.

inputs.label: Target Safety Factor (SF)
inputs.businessContext: Minimum acceptable safety factor per ISO 13822 for structural integrity.

businessContext_i18n.en: Minimum acceptable safety factor per ISO 13822 for structural integrity.

inputs.label: Support Condition
inputs.businessContext: Boundary conditions affecting deflection and moment distribution per Euler-Bernoulli theory.

businessContext_i18n.en: Boundary conditions affecting deflection and moment distribution per Euler-Bernoulli theory.

inputs.label: Enable Lean Optimization
inputs.businessContext: If true, applies Lean Six Sigma principles to minimize material waste while meeting deflection limits.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, applies Lean Six Sigma principles to minimize material waste while meeting deflection limits.

rules.message: Load position (a) must be less than beam length (L).
Uniformly distributed load (w) must be greater than 0 for UDL load type.
Point load (P) must be greater than 0 for point load types.
Young's modulus, moment of inertia, and yield strength must be positive values.
Target safety factor should be between 1.0 and 5.0 per ISO 13822.

thresholds.message: Maximum deflection exceeds L/250 limit (typical for industrial beams per ISO 9001).
Maximum bending stress exceeds yield strength — risk of plastic deformation.
Actual safety factor is below the target value. Redesign recommended.
Material utilization below 60% — consider reducing cross-section to minimize waste (Lean principle).

formulas.name: Reaction Forces
formulas.description: Calculate support reactions based on load type and position.
formulas.equation: If load_type == 'point_center': R_A = P/2, R_B = P/2. If load_type == 'point_any': R_A = P*(L-a)/L, R_B = P*a/L. If load_type == 'uniform': R_A = w*L/2, R_B = w*L/2. If load_type == 'triangular': R_A = w*L/6, R_B = w*L/3.

formulas.name: Maximum Bending Moment
formulas.description: Determine the maximum bending moment along the beam.
formulas.equation: If load_type == 'point_center': M_max = P*L/4. If load_type == 'point_any': M_max = P*a*(L-a)/L. If load_type == 'uniform': M_max = w*L^2/8. If load_type == 'triangular': M_max = w*L^2/9*sqrt(3).

formulas.name: Maximum Deflection
formulas.description: Calculate the maximum vertical deflection using Euler-Bernoulli beam theory.
formulas.equation: If load_type == 'point_center': δ_max = (P*L^3)/(48*E*I). If load_type == 'point_any': δ_max = (P*a*(L^2 - a^2)^(3/2))/(9*sqrt(3)*E*I*L). If load_type == 'uniform': δ_max = (5*w*L^4)/(384*E*I). If load_type == 'triangular': δ_max = (w*L^4)/(120*E*I).

formulas.name: Maximum Bending Stress
formulas.description: Compute the maximum bending stress from the bending moment and section modulus.
formulas.equation: σ_max = (M_max * c) / I, where c = sqrt(I/A) (assumed circular section for simplicity, but user provides I directly). For general case: σ_max = M_max * y_max / I, with y_max = (I/A)^0.5. Simplified: σ_max = M_max * 1000 / (I * 1e-8) to convert to MPa.

formulas.name: Actual Safety Factor
formulas.description: Ratio of yield strength to maximum bending stress.

formulas.name: Material Utilization
formulas.description: Efficiency of material usage based on stress ratio (Lean Six Sigma metric).

formulas.name: Lean Optimized Mass
formulas.description: If lean optimization enabled, suggest reduced cross-section to achieve target safety factor exactly.
formulas.equation: I_opt = (M_max * y_max * SF_target) / σ_y. Then mass reduction = (1 - I_opt/I) * 100%.

primary.label: Maximum Deflection
primary.description: The largest vertical displacement of the beam under the given load.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excess Material Cost
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cost of over-designed beam cross-section beyond required strength.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Deflection-Induced Downtime
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated production loss due to excessive deflection causing misalignment.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Safety Risk Premium
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional insurance or risk cost due to low safety factor.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Beam Cross-Section
suggestedActions.description: If deflection or stress exceeds limits, select a beam with higher moment of inertia (e.g., larger I-beam).

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Span Length
suggestedActions.description: Shorten the beam span by adding intermediate supports to reduce deflection.

suggestedActions.label: Use Higher Grade Material
suggestedActions.description: Upgrade to steel with higher yield strength (e.g., S355 instead of S235) to improve safety factor.

suggestedActions.label: Apply Lean Redesign
suggestedActions.description: Optimize beam cross-section to reduce material waste while meeting safety factor target.

suggestedActions.label: Implement Predictive Maintenance
suggestedActions.description: Monitor deflection trends over time to detect fatigue or creep before failure.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Adjusted confidence based on input precision and measurement uncertainty (0 to 1).
